hero.title: One pane of glass for your firewall fleet.
hero.subtitle: SNMP, sFlow, syslog, traps, and config drift monitoring across FortiGate, Palo Alto, SonicWall, pfSense, OPNsense, and Firewalla. Self-hosted. One container. 30 seconds to first poll.

why.title: Built firewall-first.
why.intro: General network monitoring tools give you a wall of generic OID graphs. Firewall-Mon is built specifically for firewalls.
why.card1_desc: Native profiles for six firewall families read CPU, sessions, VPN tunnels, HA states, and SPU/ASIC metrics out-of-the-box. Zero MIB wrestling.
why.card2_desc: Config changes are normalized to a vendor-neutral object model. Every change is classified by risk—highlighting security breaches before your audit.
why.card3_desc: Remote site probes push metrics over outbound HTTPS. No inbound ports need to be exposed, keeping your branches completely locked down.

demo.title: The Live Operations Dashboard
demo.intro: Experience the interface network security engineers use to monitor a distributed firewall fleet in real-time.
demo.initiating_poller_cycle: Initiating global poller cycle for device fleet.
demo.poller_cycle_complete_msg: Poller cycle complete for device fleet.

drift.title: Risk-Classified Config Drift
drift.intro: Every config modification is normalized to a vendor-neutral model. Our security classification system parses each diff and flags security vulnerabilities.
drift.medium_tab_desc: IPsec VPN Proposal DH Group weakened

sflow.title: sFlow v5 Flow Analyzer
sflow.intro: Triage bandwidth spikes instantly with raw conversation-level details, utilizing our PostgreSQL pgx COPY pipeline which writes 10× faster than normal ORM row inserts.
sflow.alert_text: Anomalous traffic detected on Port 4444 from host 10.200.5.88 to external IP 185.112.145.4 (Known Metasploit port). Rule matching: Suspicious_Shell_Outbound.

probe.title: Distributed Probe-Relay
probe.intro: Deploy local collectors at remote sites to sync telemetry behind CGNAT over outbound HTTPS, maintaining data parity on shaky uplinks.
probe.sg1_desc: Every packet batch is stamped with a unique, UUID-based Header. Retries on packet drops are validated at rest, preventing duplicated log analysis or traffic spikes.
probe.sg2_desc: If a remote probe and the central collector undergo a schema mismatch, `fwmon-api` flags HTTP 426 Upgrade Required. The probe's internal disk queue holds, locking your logs until safe migration completes.
probe.sg3_desc: Probe authentication keys and device passwords are hashed at rest using AES-256-GCM. Decryption verification is performed on start; pollers fail-fast immediately on key modification.

features.title: Production-grade monitoring.
features.intro: Every feature is built for speed, compliance posture, and vendor neutrality.
features.card1_desc: Six vendors. Native OID profiles. Zero MIB wrestling.
features.card2_desc: Surface a rule opened to ANY before it ships.
features.card3_desc: Top talkers, destinations, and ports — without PCAP.
features.card4_desc: Branch firewalls behind CGNAT, polled through one outbound HTTPS.
features.card6_desc: AES-256-GCM, key rotation, fail-fast keys. 0 of 170 findings open.

reports.title: Email reports, redesigned.
reports.intro: Newly styled daily digests featuring Plus Jakarta Sans and modern dark gradients—crafted to be entirely compatible with Gmail and Outlook.

security.title: 170 Findings. Zero Open.
security.intro: We subjected Firewall-Mon to a comprehensive 170-finding security audit. Every single finding has been fully addressed, patched, and verified under our 98-test static security suite.
security.item1_desc: SNMP communities, SSH credentials, and Slack webhook tokens are locked using AES-GCM encryption key hashes.
security.item2_desc: Pollers and receivers crash immediately on initialization if database encryption credentials fail to decrypt secrets. Prevents stale polling states.
security.item3_desc: All administrative actions, login events, and config backups generate immutable logs written directly to standard output.

quickstart.title: 30 seconds to first poll.
quickstart.intro: Deploy the application locally or in production with a single command.
quickstart.docker_desc: Clone the repository, configure the environment, and build the container locally (pre-built image coming soon):
quickstart.systemd_desc: Run Firewall-Mon natively as a systemd daemon. Compile the single binary using Go 1.22+:
quickstart.relay_desc: Run the lightweight Firewall-Collector on remote servers behind branch firewalls to relay SNMP data to the main server. The image is hosted on Docker Hub:

footer.brand_desc: Vendor-agnostic firewall monitoring system built for speed, security audits, and complex topology traversal.
footer.labs_created: Firewall-Mon is a TechnicalLabs project created by Xphox of Xphox Networks.
